Create file governance/validators/validate_provider_policy.py with content import json
from pathlib import Path


def validate(registry_path="governance/providers/providers_registry.json", policy_path="governance/providers/contour_provider_policy.json"):
    registry = json.loads(Path(registry_path).read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
    policy = json.loads(Path(policy_path).read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
    errors = []
    provider_ids = {p.get("id") for p in registry.get("providers", [])}
    if not provider_ids:
        errors.append("no providers registered")
    for provider in registry.get("providers", []):
        if provider.get("secrets_in_repo") is not False:
            errors.append(f"{provider.get('id')}: secrets_in_repo must be false")
    for item in policy.get("policies", []):
        if item.get("primary") not in provider_ids:
            errors.append(f"{item.get('contour')}: primary provider missing")
        if item.get("fallback") not in provider_ids:
            errors.append(f"{item.get('contour')}: fallback provider missing")
        if item.get("fallback") == "mock" and item.get("fallback_allowed_only_for") != "mock_e2e":
            errors.append(f"{item.get('contour')}: mock fallback must be mock_e2e only")
    if policy.get("external_audit_provider") not in provider_ids:
        errors.append("external audit provider missing")
    return errors

if __name__ == "__main__":
    errs = validate()
    if errs:
        print("FAIL")
        for err in errs:
            print(err)
        raise SystemExit(1)
    print("PASS provider_policy")
